channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The Gulf of Whatnow? Mapmakers grapple with Trump's geographic renaming plans
item.description: Mapmakers and teachers are re-thinking what to call the gulf of water between Mexico, the United States and Cuba after President Donald Trump ordered it renamed from the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Laurie Kellman And Hillel Italie, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Department of Justice freezes all civil rights division cases: report
item.description: The Department of Justice sent a memo to the interim director of the civil rights division, ordering a freeze to all ongoing litigation and a stop to any new cases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Everything Trump did in the first executive orders and actions of his presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump started his second administration with a blitz of policy actions to reorient the U.S. government. His executive orders cover issues that range from trade, immigration and U.S. foreign aid to demographic diversity, civil rights and the hiring of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department orders investigation of local compliance with Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has ordered federal prosecutors to investigate state or local officials who they believe are interfering with the Trump administration's crackdown on immigration, saying they could face criminal charges, in an apparent warning to the dozens of so-called sanct
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House passes immigrant detention bill that would be Trump's first law to sign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House on Wednesday gave final approval to a bill that requires the detainment of unauthorized immigrants accused of theft and violent crimes, marking the first legislation that President Donald Trump can sign as Congress, with some bipartisan support, swiftly moved in line with
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at school. Some families are now weighing school attendance
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — As President Donald Trump cracks down on immigrants in the U.S. illegally, some families are wondering if it is safe to send their children to school.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, MORIAH BALINGIT, BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress sends Laken Riley Act to Trump's desk as first bill of GOP's Washington takeover
item.description: The House of Representatives has passed the Laken Riley Act again and is sending it to President Donald Trump's desk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Danish lawmaker addressing EU tells Trump to ‘f--- off’ over Greenland bid
item.description: President Trump ruffled feathers with his plan to buy Greenland, and a Danish MEP is pushing back in a coarsely worded response put in 'words [he] might understand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon sending additional 1,500 troops to southern border: US official
item.description: The U.S. Pentagon is sending an additional 1,500 active-duty troops to the southern border by the end of the week, a senior U.S. military official told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congressional Black Caucus blasts Trump DEI action as step backward
item.description: The Congressional Black Caucus (CBC), one of the largest in Congress, blasted President Trump on Wednesday for rolling back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump made DOGE part of the government. Here's what that might mean
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The new Department of Government Efficiency, President Donald Trump ’s special commission tasked with slashing federal spending, has formally joined the government whose size it is supposed to help shrink.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump demands an apology from bishop who asked him to ‘have mercy’ on LGBTQ+ people and migrants
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday demanded an apology from the Episcopal bishop of Washington after she made a direct appeal to him during a prayer service marking his inauguration to have mercy on the LGBTQ+ community and migrant workers who are in the United States illegally.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, TIFFANY STANLEY and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. throws out policies limiting arrests of migrants at sensitive locations like schools, churches
item.description: Officers enforcing immigration laws will now be able to arrest migrants at sensitive locations like schools and churches after the Trump administration threw out policies limiting where those arrests could happen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nevada, 17 other states sue Trump administration over birthright citizenship order
item.description: Attorneys general from 18 states, including Nevada, sued the Trump administration Tuesday to stop it from seeking to eliminate birthright citizenship, a protection guaranteed in the U.S. Constitution.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump wants to pull US out of World Health Organization again; here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng, WFLA, NBC

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Court cases could shape
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court will hear arguments this week over whether the Republican-only start to session in the House is lawful. Two court decisions late last week have also set the stage for more partisan standoffs.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Taliban announce release of two Americans held in Afghanistan in a prisoner exchange
item.description: Afghanistan's Taliban government announced on Tuesday the release of two Americans in a prisoner exchange.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Immigration top priority for Trump and Florida lawmakers
item.description: Solving the illegal immigration crisis is a priority for both President Donald Trump and Florida Governor Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump issues pardons for participants in the Jan. 6 riot at the U.S. Capitol
item.description: The pardons fulfilled Trump’s promise to release supporters who tried to help him overturn his election defeat four years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms Marco Rubio as secretary of state, giving Trump the first member of his Cabinet
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate quickly confirmed Marco Rubio as secretary of state Monday, voting unanimously to give President Donald Trump the first member of his new Cabinet on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Democrats congratulate, caution Trump as he starts 2nd term
item.description: Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation -- all Democrats -- congratulated and cautioned President Donald Trump as he began his second term Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida officials react to Trump inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump was sworn in as the 47th President of the United States on Monday. Here's how Florida officials are responding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama not attending Donald Trump's inauguration: officials
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The former first lady of the U.S., Michelle Obama, isn't attending President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration on Monday, according to her office. It's not clear why she's not at the swearing-in ceremony. Her husband, former President Barack Obama, is taking part in the event.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to issue 10 executive orders cracking down on border
item.description: President-elect Trump is set to sign off on 10 executive orders Monday, moving swiftly to crack down on immigration and drug cartels during his first day in office with a blitzkrieg of immigration actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to issue executive orders defining sexes, ending DEI practices in government
item.description: President-elect Trump intends to issue executive orders on his first day in office that will recognize only two sexes, male and female, and aim to end diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices in the federal government, according to incoming White House officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams will attend Donald Trump’s inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams will attend President-elect Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday in Washington, D.C. Adams added the inauguration to his schedule Monday morning. He canceled existing appearances in New York City, including two celebrations of Martin Luther King Jr. Day.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's family circle has different look as he returns to the White House
item.description: When Donald Trump returns to the White House on Monday, his family circle will look a little different than it did when he first arrived eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa mayor meets with other U.S. mayors, responds to proposed conditions on disaster aid
item.description: Tampa Mayor Jane Castor was in Washington D.C. this weekend for the U.S. Conference of Mayors’ winter meeting. It's a time when hundreds of city leaders unite to discuss challenges and solutions involving affordable housing, infrastructure, immigration, and the climate.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-chair of President-elect Trump's new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), plans to run for governor of Ohio, two sources confirmed to The Hill on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Court aims for fall trial date in sexual misconduct lawsuit against Fletcher
item.description: The lawsuit facing Nathan Fletcher over allegations of sexual misconduct could move to a civil trial as soon as fall 2025, as both sides continue to bump heads over text messages between the ex-supervisor's accuser and a friend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: No taxes on tips comes closer to reality with Cortez Masto, Rosen push
item.description: No taxes on tips is becoming a closer reality for millions of workers after Democratic Nevada Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen and other senators reintroduced the proposal Thursday, her office said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
